title: To An Isle In The Water
content:
Shy one, Shy one,
Shy one of my heart,
She moves in the firelight
pensively apart.
She carries in the dishes,
And lays them in a row.
To an isle in the water
With her would I go.
With carries in the candles,
And lights the curtained room,
Shy in the doorway
And shy in the gloom;
And shy as a rabbit,
Helpful and shy.
To an isle in the water
With her would I fly.
meaning:
William Butler Yeats's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "To An Isle In The Water"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
